title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
summary: David Ondrej discusses the banning of Fable 5, framing it as evidence of emerging social stratification. The video appears to argue that such restrictions signal the beginning of a permanent underclass divide.
writeup: David Ondrej covers the banning of Fable 5, treating it as a case study rather than an isolated event. The argument frames the restriction as evidence of emerging social stratification, with such bans read as early signals of a permanent underclass divide. The takeaway is interpretive: Ondrej contends Fable 5 is not returning and uses that outcome to support a broader claim about hardening social tiers.
